novel_features.name: Overnight watchlist change scan
novel_features.description: See everything that changed on your tickers since you last looked — new ratings, price-target moves, breaking news, and signals in one diff.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the local ratings, news, and signal tables filtered to a ticker set and an updated-since cursor — a multi-entity diff the REST API cannot express in a single call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs a single 'what moved on my names' digest instead of fanning out across ratings, news, and signals endpoints.

novel_features.name: Single-ticker move explainer
novel_features.description: Build one time-ordered catalyst timeline for a ticker by merging unusual options, block trades, halts, rating changes, and news.
novel_features.rationale: Merges five local signal/event tables for one symbol into one chronological story; no single Benzinga endpoint returns a cross-source timeline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the question is 'why is X moving right now' and the answer needs options + halts + ratings + headlines stitched in order.

novel_features.name: Unified forward catalyst agenda
novel_features.description: One forward-dated agenda per ticker set unioning earnings, dividends, splits, IPOs, FDA dates, conference calls, guidance, and offerings.
novel_features.rationale: Unions eight calendar families that the REST API only serves one-at-a-time, keyed by (date, ticker) from the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to get every upcoming dated event on a watchlist in one ordered list rather than querying eight calendar endpoints.

novel_features.name: Analyst & firm accuracy scorecard
novel_features.description: Rank rating-issuing firms and analysts by Benzinga's historical accuracy, and tag today's rating changes with the issuer's hit rate.
novel_features.rationale: Benzinga's ratings_accuracy metric exists only as raw rows; this ranks it and left-joins the live rating-change stream so you can weight a call by its source.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent must judge whether a fresh upgrade/downgrade comes from an analyst with a real track record.

novel_features.name: Earnings surprise tracker
novel_features.description: Compute EPS and revenue beat/miss and surprise % from the earnings calendar and link each name to its conference call and transcript.
novel_features.rationale: The earnings endpoint returns estimates and actuals as separate fields with no surprise %, no beat/miss flag, and no call link; this computes and joins all three offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this during earnings season to rank the week's reports by surprise magnitude with calls/transcripts attached.

novel_features.name: Clustered congressional buying
novel_features.description: Flag tickers where several distinct members of Congress filed purchases within a window — cluster detection beyond a single disclosure.
novel_features.rationale: Groups synced congressional equity trades by ticker and counts distinct buyers — distinct-buyer cluster logic the per-row endpoint does not provide. (The SEC insider owners view carries no ticker, so clustering is scoped to the ticker-bearing congressional source.)
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to surface conviction signals where multiple unrelated members of Congress bought the same name, not just one routine filing.

narrative.headline: Every Benzinga calendar, news, fundamentals, and signal endpoint as a typed command — plus an offline SQLite store, full-text search, cross-entity queries, and the first Benzinga MCP server.
narrative.value_prop: Benzinga's licensed financial-data API is powerful but fragmented across ~60 endpoints and has only one complete client — a Python library with no CLI, no offline store, and no agent surface. This CLI covers the full documented REST surface as first-class commands, delta-syncs the calendar/news/signal families into a local database via the API's own updated cursors, and adds cross-entity commands the REST API cannot express in one call: watch a ticker set for overnight changes, explain why one name is moving, and rank analysts by accuracy.
narrative.auth_narrative: Benzinga uses a static API token passed as a query parameter. Set BENZINGA_API_KEY in your environment (or run benzinga-pp-cli auth set-token) and every command attaches it as ?token=. Access is tier-gated per product, so a valid token can still return 403 on an endpoint your plan does not include — that is a licensing boundary, not a CLI bug.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the binary and config resolve before adding a token.
Pull recent analyst rating changes for one ticker.
Build the local store so offline search and cross-entity commands work.
See everything that changed on a watchlist since yesterday.

troubleshoots.symptom: HTTP 403 on an endpoint that other commands hit fine
troubleshoots.fix: Your token works but that product is not in your Benzinga plan; this is per-endpoint licensing, not an error. Use a product your tier covers or contact Benzinga sales.
The token is missing or invalid. Set BENZINGA_API_KEY=<key> or run benzinga-pp-cli auth set-token, then benzinga-pp-cli doctor.
Those commands read the local store; run benzinga-pp-cli sync --resources ratings,news,earnings,option-activity --since 7d first.
Narrow with --tickers and --date-from, and use --select to project only the fields you need (the docs recommend narrowing every query).

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs Benzinga financial data — analyst ratings, earnings/economic/dividend calendars, breaking news, fundamentals, unusual options and other signals — as structured JSON, or when they want cross-entity views (overnight watchlist changes, a single-ticker catalyst timeline, analyst accuracy) that no single Benzinga endpoint provides. It is also the right choice for offline full-text search over synced news and calendar data.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for real-time tick/quote streaming or Squawk audio — it covers REST + delayed data, not the WebSocket/TCP live feeds.
Do not use it to place trades or manage a brokerage account — Benzinga is data-only.
Do not use it for tickers or products outside your Benzinga license; gated endpoints return 403 by design.
Do not use it as a general stock-price API for unlimited free quotes — every endpoint requires a paid Benzinga token.

recipes.title: Rank the week's earnings by surprise
recipes.explanation: Computes beat/miss and surprise % offline from synced earnings rows and projects just the decision fields.

recipes.title: Explain a sudden move
recipes.explanation: Stitches unusual options, halts, rating changes, and news for NVDA into one chronological catalyst timeline.

recipes.title: Morning watchlist diff
recipes.explanation: Cross-entity diff of ratings, news, and signals on a ticker set, narrowed to the most relevant nested fields with --select.

recipes.title: Weekly US economic calendar
recipes.explanation: Recent and latest macro releases for the US with actual vs consensus (country codes are 3-digit, e.g. USA).

recipes.title: Vet a rating change
recipes.explanation: Ranks the firms/analysts issuing fresh AAPL ratings by their historical accuracy.
